assertions.text: The vehicle was nudged by another vehicle (i.e., struck from an external direction by another vehicle).
The collision was a light tap or gentle nudge described as barely noticeable.
The driver slowed down after the nudge as a precaution (i.e., braking or reducing speed occurred after the impact).
The event occurred on a motorway.
There was a single impact (one gentle nudge) from another vehicle.

verdicts.evidence: There are impact spikes detected on multiple IMU axes around t≈[9.97](T2) with accel_x peak [−5.506 g at t=9.97 s](T2) and impact detections on accel_x, accel_y, accel_z at [t≈9.97–10.0 s](T3), which indicates the vehicle was struck from an external direction (impact spike present) [−5.506 g at t=9.97 s](T2).

The collision produced large acceleration spikes: accel_x had a peak signed deviation of [−5.506 g at t=9.97 s](T2), accel_y peak [3.754 g at t=9.98–10.0 s](T2/T3), and accel_z peak [3.78 g at t=9.92–10.0 s](T2/T3); these meet the impact-spike criterion (>|2.5 g|) in the detections [impact spikes at t≈9.97–10.0 s with magnitudes 5.51 g, 2.65 g, 3.72 g](T3), so the event is not a barely-noticeable light tap but a high-g impact [−5.506 g](T2).

GPS speed shows large drops after the impact window: there is a detected speed drop from [104.0 km/h to 87.0 km/h between t=9.0 and 12.0 s (−17.0 km/h)](T3) and further drops later (e.g., [92.0 to 67.0 km/h between t=11.0 and 14.0 s, −25.0 km/h](T3)); overall the gps speed fell from [110.0 km/h at t=0.0 s to 27.0 km/h at t=16.0 s, a −83.0 km/h change over the window](T2). The timing of the impact spikes at [t≈9.97–10.0 s](T3) precedes these speed drops, supporting that the driver slowed after the impact.

The telemetry provides no direct road-type or location metadata; GPS speed samples (first [110.0 km/h](T2)) are consistent with motorway speeds but do not prove the road type, so occurrence on a motorway is unverifiable from sensors alone.

The detector found a single clustered impact event: impact spikes across axes are co-located around [t≈9.97–10.0 s](T3) (accel_x peak at [t=9.97 s](T2), accel_y/z peaks at [t≈10.0 s](T3)) and no other impact_spike detections exist in the window, indicating one impact event.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a high-g single impact at t≈[9.97–10.0 s](T2/T3) with subsequent speed reductions, so while direction, post-impact slowing, and single-impact count are supported, the narrative claim that it was a barely-noticeable light nudge is contradicted by measured peak accelerations exceeding the impact-spike thresholds.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
